mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically increasing present values and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: A widening divergence between real GDP growth and industrial production indicates a demand-driven economic expansion that outpaces supply constraints, boosting corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a rolling 3-year regression, or SPX forward returns do not differ significantly between high-divergence and low-divergence regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated consumer price index readings force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations through higher real yields and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL rises while SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in > 50% of observations, or the correlation between CPI momentum and equity returns flips sign over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances measured by revolving credit outstanding reflect expanding household liquidity and consumption demand, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth decouples from equity returns for two consecutive quarters, or forward returns turn negative when credit balances expand above the 90th percentile historical threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in total nonfarm payrolls below the 150,000 threshold signals an imminent labor market cooling that triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in > 55% of months where PAYEMS drops below 150,000, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during the 2020-2023 period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and employment levels indicate a synchronized downturn in durable goods demand and wage-driven consumption, creating a negative feedback loop that drives down equity valuations over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline of HOUST and PAYEMS does not precede SPX drawdowns > 5% in at least 60% of historical instances, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in post-2010 data.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects recovering manufacturing capacity and easing supply bottlenecks, which lowers input costs and supports corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises while SPX 5-day forward returns remain negative in > 50% of observations, or the predictive power vanishes when supply chain pressure indices are simultaneously elevated.
